name: yj_nearbyglasses
description: attempting to detect smart glasses nearby and warn you
readme_summary: This Android application detects nearby smart glasses by analyzing their Bluetooth advertising data. It aims to provide users with a privacy-focused warning system, alerting them if potentially surveillance-capable smart glasses are in their vicinity. The app emphasizes user privacy by not collecting or transmitting any personal data.
key_features: Detects nearby smart glasses via Bluetooth LE advertising
Warns users of potential smart glasses presence
Privacy-focused: no data collection or telemetry
Free and open-source
Local log file storage (optional)
primary_use_case: To alert users about the presence of nearby smart glasses, acting as a privacy and self-defense tool.
cover_image_prompt: A lone individual walking through a bustling city street, subtly holding up a smartphone. The smartphone screen displays a stylized, glowing radar interface with distinct 'ping' icons appearing around people wearing modern, sleek glasses. These 'ping' icons are visually distinct from regular people, perhaps emitting a faint, technological aura. The background is slightly blurred to focus on the interaction between the phone and the detected glasses. Small, almost imperceptible digital particles emanate from the detected glasses, hinting at their 'smart' nature. The overall mood is one of awareness and subtle caution. The art style should be a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear iconography and a slightly futuristic, yet grounded, aesthetic. The repository name 'Nearby Glasses' could be subtly integrated into a UI element on the phone screen.

name: Edit-Banana
description: Edit Banana: A framework for converting statistical formats into editable.
readme_summary: Edit Banana is a framework that converts static visual formats, such as figures and diagrams from images, into fully editable formats like DrawIO (XML). It leverages advanced AI models, including SAM 3 and multimodal large models, to reconstruct diagrams with high fidelity, preserving original details and logical relationships.
key_features: Converts static images of diagrams into editable DrawIO (XML) format
High-fidelity reconstruction preserving original details and logical relationships
Powered by SAM 3 and multimodal large models
Online demo available for immediate use
Supports various diagram types including flowcharts and architecture diagrams
primary_use_case: Transforming non-editable visual diagrams from images into editable formats for easy modification and reuse.
cover_image_prompt: A magical workbench where a skilled artisan is carefully transforming a flat, printed blueprint of a complex machine into a fully assembled, 3D mechanical model. The blueprint is made of paper, while the resulting model is glowing with intricate, interconnected parts. Digital particles and subtle UI elements representing AI processing float around the artisan's hands. The repository name 'Edit Banana' is subtly etched onto the workbench. The scene is brightly lit with a clean, modern aesthetic, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on clear visual transformation and a touch of technological magic.

name: device-activity-tracker
description: A phone number can reveal whether a device is active, in standby or offline (and more). This PoC demonstrates how delivery receipts + RTT timing leak sensitive device-activity patterns. (WhatsApp / Signal)
readme_summary: This project demonstrates a proof-of-concept for tracking device activity on WhatsApp and Signal by analyzing the Round-Trip Time (RTT) of message delivery receipts. It can infer whether a device is active, in standby, or offline, and potentially detect location changes.
key_features: Detects device activity (active, standby, offline) based on RTT of delivery receipts.
Infers potential location changes by differentiating between mobile data and WiFi.
Provides a web interface for real-time monitoring and activity pattern visualization.
Offers a CLI interface for direct interaction.
Utilizes Node.js, TypeScript, and React for its implementation.
Includes Docker support for easy deployment.
primary_use_case: Security research and demonstration of privacy vulnerabilities in instant messaging applications like WhatsApp and Signal by analyzing device activity patterns through RTT measurements.
cover_image_prompt: A digital detective, represented as a stylized silhouette with glowing data streams emanating from their head, is carefully observing a series of interconnected, abstract representations of mobile phones. Each phone icon subtly shifts color and intensity, indicating its 'activity' status (e.g., bright and pulsing for active, dim and slow for standby, dark for offline). The detective holds a magnifying glass that focuses on the 'delivery receipt' pathway between phones, with a visible timer or speed gauge showing varying RTT values. Small, almost translucent UI elements displaying 'RTT', 'Status: Active/Standby', and 'Time' float around the scene. The background is a dark, abstract network grid, with subtle code snippets and data points flowing around. The overall mood is one of quiet, analytical observation. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a futuristic, tech-noir aesthetic, emphasizing clarity and the flow of information.

name: DeArrow
description: Crowdsourcing better titles and thumbnails on YouTube
readme_summary: DeArrow is a browser extension that aims to combat sensationalism on YouTube by allowing users to crowdsource and submit better titles and thumbnails. It replaces original YouTube titles and thumbnails with user-submitted alternatives or applies configurable formatting and random screenshots when no submissions are available.
key_features: Crowdsourced titles and thumbnails for YouTube videos
Replaces original titles with user-submitted or formatted text
Replaces original thumbnails with user-submitted screenshots or random timestamps
Configurable fallback options for titles and thumbnails
Option to view original titles and thumbnails
Browser extension available for Chrome, Firefox, and Safari
primary_use_case: Improving the accuracy and reducing the sensationalism of YouTube video titles and thumbnails through community contributions and configurable display options.
cover_image_prompt: A wise librarian in a bustling digital library, meticulously curating book covers (representing YouTube videos). The librarian is replacing generic, flashy covers with more informative and accurate ones submitted by patrons (represented by glowing icons of user contributions). Some books have their titles being rewritten on elegant scrolls. In the background, a subtle screen displays DeArrow's logo and a feed of user-submitted title/thumbnail suggestions. The scene is brightly lit with a clean, modern aesthetic, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing clarity and organization.

name: homepage-lite
description: Homepage dashboard for managing and monitoring your homelab services and bookmarks.
readme_summary: Homepage-lite is a lightweight, Go-powered dashboard for managing and monitoring homelab services and bookmarks. It offers real-time status checks, system metrics, and a customizable modern UI with multiple themes.
key_features: Service Monitoring with real-time status checks
Bookmark Management organized by groups
System Metrics (CPU, Memory, Disk) with real-time updates
Modern UI with multiple themes and custom backgrounds
Lightweight design with a single binary and low memory footprint
primary_use_case: To provide a centralized, easy-to-manage dashboard for homelab services, bookmarks, and system monitoring.
cover_image_prompt: A digital control panel resembling a futuristic spaceship's main console, displaying real-time status indicators for various interconnected services (represented by glowing icons like servers, routers, and smart home devices). In the center, a holographic display shows organized bookmarks as clickable tiles. Subtle CPU and memory usage graphs pulse gently in a corner. The overall mood is organized, efficient, and modern, with a clean, dark background and vibrant, glowing UI elements. The repository name 'Homepage-lite' is subtly integrated into a digital readout on the console. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clarity and a slightly stylized, futuristic aesthetic.
